danger_signals.visual_form: Boss character appearance at tournament finale
danger_signals.meaning: Ultimate threat/nemesis - represents criminal underworld danger and personal vendetta
Secondary threat level - enforcer/lieutenant of criminal power structure
Escalating danger - each victory brings closer confrontation with primary antagonist
Lawless zone controlled by criminal elements - inherent danger context

safety_signals.meaning: Strength through companionship - Terry and Andy together represent stability

safety_signals.visual_form: Allied third character in tournament team
safety_signals.meaning: Expanded support network - adds capability and mutual protection

safety_signals.visual_form: Special move execution reflecting learned techniques
safety_signals.meaning: Preparation and legitimate skill - Andy's Japan training and Terry's Hakkyokuseiken training represent readiness
Incremental progression and capability building toward final confrontation

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Hakkyokuseiken martial art style / street fighting fusion
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: South Town streets as home territory; Japan connection via Andy

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: King of Fighters tournament hosting - corrupted martial arts competition as control mechanism
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Geese as crime boss antagonist; Billy Kane as enforcer lieutenant
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: South Town criminal underworld domain - Geese 'rules' this space as merciless crime boss

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Hakkyokuseiken fighting art - legitimate martial tradition
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Murdered by Geese - represents legitimate order destroyed by criminal power
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Jeff's grave as pilgrimage point; mentor Tung Fu Rue connection

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Muay Thai expertise - distinct martial tradition
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Thailand origin; Japanese connection through South Town arrival

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: South Town operates under Geese Howard's ruthless rule; tournament exists as spectacle of power
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Characters operate as individuals seeking entry; Geese controls tournament structure and finals access

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Tournament progression: Challenge to criminal authority
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Successive opponent defeats represent penetration of Geese's tournament hierarchy
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player must advance through brackets; CPU opponents block progression; two-lane battlefield mechanics allow tactical positioning

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Final confrontation: Direct challenge to power structure
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Geese Howard as final boss - direct personal and political confrontation
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Vengeance oath completion; avenging Jeff Bogard's murder represents restoration of legitimate martial order against criminal usurpation

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Character skill levels reflect training intensity; special move mastery indicates readiness
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Andy trained in Japan with Shiranui-ryū Ninjutsu; Terry combined street fighting with Hakkyokuseiken; both manifest distinct fighting styles

environmental_storytelling.example: South Town environment visually communicates criminal underworld control - Geese's domain and tournament venue conflated
Jeff Bogard's murder by Geese establishes moral stakes - legitimate martial master destroyed by ruthless businessman, justifying vengeance narrative
King of Fighters tournament visually organizes power structure - Geese at apex, Billy Kane as lieutenant, player characters as challengers ascending through brackets

environmental_storytelling.technique: Martial arts style as cultural identity
environmental_storytelling.example: Character movesets reflect training locations and mentors - Hakkyokuseiken (legitimate lineage), Shiranui-ryū Ninjutsu (Japanese discipline), Muay Thai (Joe's origin) communicate faction allegiances and philosophical positions
Two-row stages (background/foreground positioning) reflect tactical complexity - physical space becomes strategic element rather than purely visual

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Wandering in home country, combining street techniques with formal Hakkyokuseiken training - active preparation phase
Returns from Japan mastery of Shiranui-ryū Ninjutsu - readiness signal through geographic return
Organizes KOF tournament as display of power - controls both martial competition and criminal underworld simultaneously
Final boss position indicates enforcer role - direct protector of criminal hierarchy
Joins Bogard brothers in tournament - alliance formation indicates coalition against criminal power structure
Arm wrestling mini-game requires rapid button input - mechanical test of readiness between tournament matches

notes: Fatal Fury communicates world-state primarily through narrative framing and character positioning rather than dynamic environmental change. The two-lane battle system is the primary mechanical manifestation of strategic positioning. Geese Howard embodies both criminal political power (organized crime boss) and martial arts mastery, making him a unified symbolic antagonist. The tournament structure serves double purpose: spectacle of entertainment and mechanism of power consolidation. Character special moves derived from martial lineages (Hakkyokuseiken, Shiranui-ryū, Muay Thai) function as visual faction indicators. The Bogard brothers' decade-long training oath represents preparation within legitimate martial tradition against illegitimate criminal authority - a philosophical conflict manifested through fighting game mechanics.
sources_used: wikipedia - Fatal Fury: King of Fighters (plot, gameplay, character descriptions, narrative structure)
